# AI Description

# Technical Description of Figure A-4.2.2.3.-B

## 1. Diagram Type
This is a technical architectural detail drawing consisting of two views: a **section view** (top) and a **plan view** (bottom) that illustrate the spatial relationships and dimensional requirements for calculating primary and secondary sidelighted areas in accordance with NECB daylighting provisions.

## 2. Building Components and Systems Shown
- **Vertical glazing systems** (windows)
- **Primary sidelighted floor areas** (areas directly adjacent to windows)
- **Secondary sidelighted floor areas** (areas adjacent to primary sidelighted areas)
- **Vertical obstructions** (walls, partitions, or other barriers ≥1.5 m in height)
- **Opaque breaks between windows**
- **Building envelope elements** (exterior walls, interior partitions)

## 3. Visible Labels, Measurements, and Annotations

### Section View (Top):
- **"Section View"** - orientation label
- **"top of glazing"** - marks the upper limit of the window
- **"head height (HH)"** - vertical dimension from floor to top of glazing
- **"Obstruction is 1.5 m or higher"** - notation indicating qualifying vertical barriers
- **"primary sidelighted area (1 × HH)"** - floor area with depth equal to one head height
- **"secondary sidelighted area (1 × HH)"** - floor area extending beyond primary area with depth equal to one head height

### Plan View (Bottom):
- **"Plan View"** - orientation label
- **"½ × HH"** - dimension shown at both top and bottom of window, indicating lateral extension of secondary sidelighted area equal to half the head height
- **"Opaque break between windows is less than 1 × HH wide"** - notation describing the separation between adjacent windows
- **"primary sidelighted area"** - shaded area directly in front of window
- **"secondary sidelighted area"** - shaded area surrounding primary area
- **"Obstruction is 1.5 m or higher"** - notation indicating qualifying vertical barrier that limits secondary sidelighted area

## 4. Relationships and Connections Between Elements

### Section View Relationships:
- The **head height (HH)** is the fundamental measurement unit, defined as the vertical distance from the floor to the top of glazing
- The **primary sidelighted area** extends perpendicular from the glazing to a depth of **1 × HH**
- The **secondary sidelighted area** begins at the edge of the primary sidelighted area and extends an additional **1 × HH** depth
- **Vertical obstructions ≥1.5 m** terminate both primary and secondary sidelighted areas where encountered

### Plan View Relationships:
- The **primary sidelighted area** is centered on the window width
- The **secondary sidelighted area** extends laterally beyond the primary area by **½ × HH** on each side of the window
- When **opaque breaks between windows are less than 1 × HH wide**, the sidelighted areas from adjacent windows can overlap (areas are combined without double-counting per code requirements)
- **Vertical obstructions ≥1.5 m** limit the lateral extent of secondary sidelighted areas
- The secondary sidelighted area wraps around the primary area on three sides (both lateral sides and the side opposite the glazing)

## 5. Compliance Requirements and Technical Specifications

### Dimensional Requirements:
- **Primary sidelighted area depth**: 1 × head height (HH) measured perpendicular from glazing
- **Secondary sidelighted area depth**: 1 × head height (HH) measured from the edge of primary sidelighted area
- **Secondary sidelighted area lateral width**: ½ × head height (HH) on each side of window, or to obstruction, whichever is less

### Obstruction Criteria:
- Only **vertical obstructions 1.5 m or higher** qualify as boundaries that limit sidelighted areas
- Obstructions below 1.5 m height do not interrupt the calculation of sidelighted areas

### Area Calculation Rules:
- **No double-counting**: Where sidelighted areas from multiple windows overlap, the combined area is calculated without counting overlapping portions multiple times
- **Opaque breaks**: When the distance between adjacent windows is less than 1
